code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of building‑related triples into a fluent sentence."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Helper: map predicates to short natural‑language fragments.
    phrase_map = {
        "architecturalStyle": lambda s, o: f"has an architectural style of {o}",
        "buildingStartDate": lambda s, o: f"started construction in {o}",
        "completionDate":   lambda s, o: f"was completed in {o}",
        "floorCount":       lambda s, o: f"has {o} floors",
        "location":         lambda s, o: f"is located at {o}",
        "cost":             lambda s, o: f"costs {o}",
        "floorArea":        lambda s, o: f"covers a floor area of {o}",
        "architect":        lambda s, o: f"was designed by {o}",
        "owner":            lambda s, o: f"is owned by {o}",
        "formerName":       lambda s, o: f"was formerly known as {o}",
        "height":           lambda s, o: f"reaches a height of {o}",
        "buildingType":    lambda s, o: f"is a {o}",
        "developer":        lambda s, o: f"was developed by {o}",
        "tenant":           lambda s, o: f"hosts the tenant {o}",
        "isPartOf":         lambda s, o: f"is part of {o}",
        "country":          lambda s, o: f"is in {o}",
        "currentTenants":   lambda s, o: f"currently houses {o}",
        "address":          lambda s, o: f"has the address {o}",
        "inaugurationDate": lambda s, o: f"was inaugurated on {o}",
        "birthPlace":       lambda s, o: f"was born in {o}",
        "deathPlace":       lambda s, o: f"died in {o}",
        "bedCount":         lambda s, o: f"has {o} beds",
        "region":           lambda s, o: f"is in the {o} region",
        "state":            lambda s, o: f"is located in {o}",
        "website":          lambda s, o: f"has a website at {o}",
        "yearOfConstruction": lambda s, o: f"was built in {o}",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": lambda s, o: f"has NRHP reference number {o}",
        "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": lambda s, o: f"was added to the NRHP on {o}",
        "significantBuilding": lambda s, o: f"is known for the significant building {o}",
        "governingBody":    lambda s, o: f"is governed by {o}",
        "leader":           lambda s, o: f"is led by {o}",
        "chancellor":       lambda s, o: f"has chancellor {o}",
        "governmentType":   lambda s, o: f"has a government type of {o}",
        "capital":          lambda s, o: f"has capital {o}",
        "language":         lambda s, o: f"speaks {o}",
        "leaderTitle":      lambda s, o: f"has leader title {o}",
        "currency":         lambda s, o: f"uses the {o}",
        "ethnicGroup":      lambda s, o: f"has ethnic group {o}",
        "origin":           lambda s, o: f"originates from {o}",
        "significantProject": lambda s, o: f"is known for the project {o}",
        "foundationPlace":  lambda s, o: f"was founded in {o}",
        "demonym":          lambda s, o: f"has demonym {o}",
        "numberOfRooms":    lambda s, o: f"contains {o} rooms",
        "keyPerson":        lambda s, o: f"has key person {o}",
        "architecture":     lambda s, o: f"features architecture {o}",
        "postalCode":       lambda s, o: f"has postal code {o}",
    }

    # Group triples by subject to build sentences per entity.
    subjects = {}
    for t in triples:
        subjects.setdefault(t.subject, []).append(t)

    sentences = []
    for subj, tlist in subjects.items():
        fragments = []
        for t in tlist:
            phr_func = phrase_map.get(t.predicate, lambda s, o: f"{t.predicate} {o}")
            fragments.append(phr_func(subj, t.object))

        # Build a fluent description: first fragment as main clause, rest as commas.
        if fragments:
            description = f"{subj} " + fragments[0]
            if len(fragments) > 1:
                description += ", " + ", ".join(fragments[1:])
            description += "."
            sentences.append(description)

    return " ".join(sentences)

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
